Title: The PPR protein RARE1-mediated editing of chloroplast accD transcripts is required for fatty acid biosynthesis and heat tolerance in Arabidopsis

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Alpha-carboxyltransferase subunit of plastid heteromeric ACCase
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Reduced expression in cac3-1 due to T-DNA insertion in 3' UTR neighboring sequence
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Unsaturated fatty acids maintain membrane integrity and heat tolerance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation of CAC3 causes dwarfism specifically under heat stress, supporting that plastid ACCase activity is required for heat tolerance and fatty acid-associated membrane homeostasis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant cac3-1; CRISPR-Cas9 mutant cac3-2; heat-stress phenotyping
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Maintaining CAC3 function is important when engineering plastid ACCase to improve heat resilience
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CAC3 encodes the alpha-carboxyltransferase subunit of plastid ACCase. 2. cac3 mutants are near wild type at 22°C but are dwarf under heat stress. 3. CAC3 genetic evidence supports that impaired plastid ACCase underlies heat sensitivity linked to altered fatty acid biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used mainly as supporting genetic evidence for ACCase involvement rather than the primary molecular focus of the study.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Acetyl-CoA carboxylase beta-carboxyl transferase subunit (AccD); chloroplast-encoded ACCase subunit
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions as the beta-carboxyltransferase subunit of plastid heteromeric ACCase that catalyzes conversion of acetyl-CoA to malonyl-CoA in the first committed step of fatty acid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: C794-edited accD promotes ACCase activity, increases C18:1 and C18:2 accumulation, maintains membrane integrity, and enhances heat tolerance; loss of C794 editing reduces these UFAs and causes yellow dwarf heat-sensitive plants
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Unsaturated fatty acids help maintain membrane structural homeostasis, fluidity, and heat tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Heat treatment increased accD-C794 editing rate; accD overexpression lines were analyzed under heat stress
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heat-responsive through increased C794 editing under heat treatment
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive) for edited accD overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Heat stress at 28°C; exogenous malonyl-CoA; exogenous C18:1 and C18:2
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: GC-MS fatty acid profiling; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: rare1 loss-of-function mutants abolishing accD-C794 editing; edited accD overexpression in rare1 and WT; chlorophyll and growth phenotyping under heat; ACCase activity assay; fatty acid profiling; malonyl-CoA rescue; TEM
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Engineering expression of the edited accD form can enhance heat tolerance and increase seed fatty acid content, particularly C18:1 and C18:2
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. accD encodes the beta-carboxyltransferase subunit of plastid ACCase and supports the committed step of fatty acid biosynthesis. 2. Loss of C794 RNA editing lowers ACCase activity under heat stress and decreases C18:1 and C18:2 levels. 3. Overexpression of the C794-edited accD form enhances heat tolerance and elevates seed fatty acid content.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The key functional variable studied is RNA editing at accD-C794 causing an S265L substitution in AccD; loss of editing has little effect under normal temperature but is critical under heat stress.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Precursor supply for fatty acid biosynthesis via chloroplast RNA editing of accD
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Post-transcriptional RNA editing activation of chloroplast accD transcript at C794
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes C-to-U editing of accD transcripts, enabling functional edited accD expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential activator of accD-C794 editing required for ACCase function under heat stress and thus for UFA accumulation and heat tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Maintain membrane integrity/fluidity and support heat tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of RARE1 abolishes accD-C794 editing, reduces ACCase activity and C18:1/C18:2 accumulation under heat stress, and causes yellow dwarf heat-sensitive plants
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: RARE1 expression increases during heat treatment
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Heat stress at 28°C; malonyl-CoA supplementation; exogenous C18:1 and C18:2
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: GC-MS fatty acid profiling; no public accession reported
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout mutants rare1-1 and rare1-2; complementation with pRARE1::RARE1-c-Myc; accD editing assay by sequencing; heat-stress phenotyping; ACCase assay; fatty acid profiling; rescue experiments
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating RARE1 or accD editing efficiency could improve heat tolerance by sustaining plastid fatty acid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RARE1 is required for C-to-U editing of chloroplast accD at site C794. 2. rare1 mutants are normal at 22°C but become yellow and dwarf at 28°C because ACCase activity and C18:1/C18:2 levels decline. 3. RARE1 expression and accD editing rise with heat treatment, linking this RNA-editing regulator to heat adaptation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although RARE1 is an RNA-editing factor rather than a classical transcription factor, it is a core regulator in this study because it causally controls the edited accD state underlying the metabolic phenotype.
